title: Top Five Christopher Nolan Movies and a ‘Tenet’ Review From Canada
format: Sean, Amanda, and critic Adam Nayman argue through the Christopher Nolan filmography and each name their favorites, then Nayman, watching from Toronto ahead of the U.S. release, reviews Tenet spoiler-free.

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: Adam's favorite piece of Nolan criticism isn't criticism at all: a Vulture item about his kids calling him Reynolds Woodcock for six months.

films.title: Memento
films.note: Adam rewatched it early in the pandemic and noticed it's funny on purpose, in Guy Pearce's ‘Am I chasing this guy? Is he chasing me?’

films.title: Inception
films.note: The episode's longest tangent: the memes, and ‘incepted’ turning up as a verb in a novel Amanda's reading, have it existing outside the movie itself now.

films.title: The Dark Knight Rises
films.note: Adam hasn't thought about it since he sat through it, aside from the Bane-inaudibility jokes.

films.title: Insomnia
films.note: Sean floated it for his own top five, maybe the one Nolan film without that sense of something coming, then talked himself out of it as too contrarian a pick.

films.title: Batman Begins
films.note: On nobody's list. Adam calls it an early, table-setting entry in the world-building game Marvel would later industrialize.

segments.heading: Sean Fennessey's Top Five Nolan Movies
segments.subject: Nolan movies

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: Still his No. 1: ‘the electrifying feeling I like in a mainstream movie,’ flaws and all.

films.title: Memento
films.note: More clever, and funnier, than the one above it, he says.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: Grown on him more than any other Nolan film since he first saw it.

films.title: The Prestige
films.note: One he's happy to revisit.

films.title: Interstellar
films.note: Doesn't crack his top tier but has real strengths, he thinks.

segments.heading: Amanda Dobbins' Top Five Nolan Movies
segments.subject: Nolan movies

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: A rewatch the night before confirmed it: the first 90 minutes are as good as blockbuster filmmaking gets.

films.title: Inception
films.note: Her known minority position; she says she's the only one still fond of it.

films.title: Memento
films.note: Actual household shorthand: she and her husband invoke the tattoo trick over his memory.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: Nolan's solemn, serious side executed at its best, she says, and genuinely moving.

segments.heading: Adam Nayman's Top Five Nolan Movies
segments.subject: Nolan movies

films.title: The Prestige
films.note: His favorite ‘by a lot,’ a fatalistic movie about entertainers destroying each other and themselves, which he half-jokingly calls Nolan's most personal work.

films.title: Memento
films.note: A leaner, less self-serious version of the same forward drive he finds in The Prestige.

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: One of the three films all three panelists share.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: The other of the three consensus picks.

films.title: Tenet
films.note: Adam's verdict: didn't fully understand it, didn't quite feel it either, but had a good time anyway. Strong first hour, shakier last ninety minutes. Plays closest to a Bond movie of anything in Nolan's catalog, dialogue famously buried in the sound mix, and he doesn't think it's a spoiler-landmark movie the way The Usual Suspects or The Sixth Sense are.

films.title: The Killing
films.note: Kubrick's heist film with the clown masks. Adam figures Nolan loves it enough to have remade it twice, as Inception and now Tenet.

films.title: Crash (1996)
films.note: The last movie Adam saw in a theater before Tenet, at a drive-in. Didn't tear up for that one; he did for Tenet.
